attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: On/off state; reflects to `aria-checked` true/false. A switch is strictly binary — no mixed state.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control; excluded from form submission and the tab order.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Form field name the on/off value is submitted under (native `<input type="checkbox">`).

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value submitted when the switch is on.

properties.name: checked
properties.description: Gets or sets the on/off state; the change takes effect immediately (distinct from a checkbox’s deferred form value).

name: Toggle / Switch
summary: Native-first over `<input type="checkbox">` with `role="switch"`: a boolean on/off control whose change takes effect IMMEDIATELY, distinct from a checkbox's deferred-until-submit form value. Realizes the `input` boolean control. `aria-checked` true/false reflects on/off; no mixed/indeterminate state (a switch is strictly binary). Zero JS — the native element plus `role="switch"` and styling IS the block. Sliced from #468 (form-control inventory).
designDecisions: Native-first: a switch is the platform `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">` (or a `<button aria-pressed>` for a pure action) — no custom element. The block standardizes the role + label + immediate-effect contract, not a new widget.

Immediate vs. deferred is THE distinction from checkbox (#472): a switch applies its state on toggle (e.g. enable a setting now); a checkbox is a form value committed on submit. Same `<input type=checkbox>` substrate, different role and timing — separate blocks, never a checkbox `mode` flag.

Strictly binary: a switch has no `indeterminate`/`mixed` state (unlike the checkbox). `aria-checked` is true/false only; the WAI-ARIA APG Switch pattern with `<kbd>Space</kbd>` toggle and an on/off accessible name.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Native `<input type="checkbox">` with `role="switch"`: a boolean on/off control whose change takes effect immediately; Space/Enter toggle `aria-checked`.
